title: Dia
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Human/Legends|Human]]|[[Dia Passik]], a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]]}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Dia-KOTOR.jpg]] |name=Dia |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]]Game files from ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' establish Dia as having a [[Gender/Legends|female]] head model. |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Javyar's Cantina]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Who are you? What are you doing in here? You can't just come barging into someone's home!|Dia, to Revan|}} '''Dia''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. She worked as a [[Waiter/Legends|waitress]] at [[Javyar's Cantina]], located in Taris's [[Lower City]]. While employed at the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], Dia was harassed by a [[Alcohol/Legends|drunk]] man named [[Holdan]], who worked for the [[Exchange]]'s local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], [[Davik Kang]]. In retaliation, Dia struck Holdan with a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]], injuring and humiliating him. Holdan responded by putting a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Dia's head at the [[Zax's bounty office|bounty office]] run by [[Zax]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], another of Kang's associates. Dia was forced into hiding in an [[Upper City]] [[South Apartments|apartment]], where she lost touch with the outside world. [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], a former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] whose memories had been [[Amnesia/Legends|lost]], arrived on Taris and met Dia in her apartment. Dia was convinced to tell Revan her situation, and Revan agreed to help remove the bounty. Revan later encountered Holdan in Javyar's Cantina, where he persuaded him to remove the bounty. Revan returned to Dia, informing her of his success, and she was able to come out of hiding. ==Biography== ===Confrontation with Holdan=== {{Quote|She used to be a waitress in the Cantina here until she had fight with Holdan. She cut him with her vibroblade, almost killed him. That was a mistake.|Dia, to Revan|}} [[File:Holdan.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Holdan, an associate of Davik Kang, drunkenly assaulted Dia.]] Dia lived on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. She was relatively poor and worked as a [[Waiter/Legends|waitress]] at [[Javyar's Cantina]] in the [[Lower City]] of the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]]. During Dia's employment at the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], she encountered [[Holdan]], a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]Game files from ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' establish Holdan as having a [[Gender/Legends|male]] head model. who worked for [[Davik Kang]], the local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] for the criminal organization known as the [[Exchange]]. Holdan became [[Alcohol/Legends|drunk]] and attempted to force himself on Dia as she worked. She loudly spurned Holdan's advances and defended herself with a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]], nearly [[Death/Legends|killing]] him. Holdan received a scar from the confrontation and was humiliated in front of the people attending the cantina with him. Out of anger and in an attempt to maintain his reputation, Holdan put out an illegal [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of three hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on Dia at a [[Zax's bounty office|bounty office]], located within the same cantina, that was owned by [[Zax]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], another associate of Kang and the Exchange. ===In hiding=== {{Quote|Holdan's a spiteful little Hutt-slug. He went and put out a bounty on my head for what I did. That's why I'm hiding out here.|Dia, to Revan|}} [[File:DiaApartment.png|thumb|right|320px|Dia was forced to hide out in her Upper City apartment.]] Dia was forced to end her employment as a waitress and fled to the planet's [[Upper City]] and went into hiding. She eventually found an apartment in the run-down [[South Apartments]] ring, amongst illegal [[Alien/Legends|aliens]] forced to live there by Taris's [[Speciesism|speciesist]] laws. Because of Holdan's connections to Kang and the Exchange, the [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|local authorities]] turned a blind eye. She barely made enough [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to survive, and thus was unable to pay off the three hundred credit bounty herself. Too afraid for her life to leave the apartment, Dia soon lost touch with the political affairs of the outside world. ===Removing the bounty=== In [[3956 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]],'' which includes [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] interactions with Dia and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith]] quarantine of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], in [[3956 BBY]]. following a [[Attack on the Endar Spire|battle]] [[Orbit/Legends|above]] the planet between the Republic [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] ''[[Endar Spire]]'' and a [[Sith armada (Jedi Civil War)|Sith fleet]], several Republic [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]] crashed onto the surface. One of these pods contained the Republic war hero [[Carth Onasi]] and the former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], whose memories were replaced with those of a loyal [[Republic soldier]]. Revan and Onasi took refuge in the Upper City's South Apartments, attempting to stay out of sight of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], who had imposed a planet-wide [[Blockade/Legends|quarantine]] on Taris in an attempt to caputre [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Bastila Shan|Basitla Shan]], another survivor of the battle hose [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] [[Force power/Legends|ability]] made her an important part of the Republic's war effort. The duo's hideout was next door to Dia's apartment, which was kept locked. Revan found his way through her locked door and approached Dia, who was surprised and berated him for bursting in. Despite his apologies, she remained upset, though she noted he was more polite than Holdan. Revan asked about Holdan, but Dia hesitated to give much information, as she didn't trust him. However, Revan was able to convince Dia to tell the full story, and she explained her plight to him. Revan offered to help remove the bounty, and though Dia doubted he could, she directed him to Javyar's Cantina, where Holdan enjoyed spending time. Dia wished Revan luck on talking sense into Holdan, but was unable to answer Revan's other questions, as she had been sheltered inside her apartment. Revan found and confronted Holdan at Javyar's Cantina. Though Holdan was reluctant to remove the bounty, wishing to save his reputation, Revan and his party were able to convince him otherwise.{{CSWECite|II|49|Holdan}} Holdan went to the bounty office called off the bounty with [[Zax]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], who was also an associate of Davik Kang. {{GameLSmechanics}} Revan returned to Dia's apartment to tell her of his success. Upon hearing the news, she was overjoyed and thanked him profusely. While she didn't have much, Dia offered to reward Revan with an [[Energy projector/Legends|Energy projecter]], used to upgrade [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapons]], which was family heirloom. However, Revan refused the reward, claiming that helping her was all the thanks he needed. The woman offered a final goodbye and thanks to Revan, and was able to come out of hiding. {{Endgame}} ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|That's no excuse. You can't just go around barging into people's apartments because you're curious! But at least you're more polite than that pig, Holdan.''"
"''Holdan? Who's that?''"
"''Just one of Davik's men who can't keep his hands to himself. But all he got for his trouble was a nasty scar from my vibroblade. Too bad I'm still the one paying the price.|Dia, to Revan|}} Dia was a light-skinned [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] with [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. When Dia received a bounty on her head, she believed the most sensible course was to hide away in her apartment, refusing to risk leaving the complex. Though this was motivated by fear, when attacked or faced with unavoidable danger, she believed in—and was capable of—making a brave stand to prove she was not as defenseless as she seemed. She could be defiant and did not shy away from criticizing the misdeeds of others, such as burglary or harassment. ==Equipment== Dia wore a green tunic with a gray [[Clothing/Legends|undertunic]] and gray pants. She also had brown knee length [[Boot/Legends|boots]], dark brown [[Glove/Legends|gloves]], and a metallic [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]] with several pouches. She wore her hair in a ponytail secured with a brown tie. During her work as a waitress, she carried a vibroblade on her person, which she used against Holdan. ==Behind the scenes== Dia first appears as a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', released by [[BioWare]]. Player interactions with her are optional, though her dialogue can start the "Dia's Bounty" side quest. This quest may be fulfilled in several ways, changing the players alignment accordingly. In order to start the quest, the player must pass a persuasion check or threaten Dia. Alternatively, the player may refuse or avoid the quest entirely. The [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' confirms that Revan completed the quest, stating that Revan convinced Holdan to remove the bounty. If the player speaks to Dia while dressed in [[Sith trooper armor/Legends|Sith armor]], she expresses annoyance with the Sith patrols, and tells Revan that he will find nothing in her apartment. ===Gameplay alternatives=== ====Light side options==== {{Quote|He... he called the bounty off? That's wonderful! I only wish I had something to give you as a reward.|Dia to Revan after hearing about the removal of her bounty|}} There are three ways to convince Holdan to drop the bounty and receive [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] points. The player can pay him two hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to cancel the bounty or persuade him to cancel without paying. If [[Bastila Shan]] is in the party during the confrontation, she will use [[Mind trick/Legends|Force persuasion]] to have him withdraw the bounty should the player fail to convince Holdan. If the player has Dia's bounty removed, she will offer the player an Energy projector. The player can either accept it or either refuse and obtain more light side points. ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' does not specify which of these options was taken. ====Dark side options==== {{Quote|Then you're going to learn the same lesson I taught Holdan: I'm not as helpless as I look!|Dia after being threatened by Revan|}} In an alternative [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] option, the player can kill Dia to claim the bounty. If Carth Onasi is in the party, he objects to the act, giving the player chance to reconsider the choice. The player may decide to spare her or proceed with the attack. If Dia is killed, the player can collect a three hundred credit bounty from Zax in the Lower City. The player may persuade Zax to raise the reward to four hundred credits. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{CSWECite|II|49|Holdan}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Dia#legends |nl=Dia |pl=Dia }} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Tarisians]] [[Category:Waiters]]
